name: Switzerland
intro: Switzerland concentrates an extraordinary density of project work: pharmaceutical giants running global drug-development programmes from Basel, banks in Zurich and Geneva under permanent regulatory transformation, and international organisations coordinating multi-country initiatives. Salaries for certified professionals are the highest in Europe, and Swiss employers — precise by culture — treat certification as baseline evidence rather than decoration. PMP and Scrum credentials both carry full recognition. You can join a live online cohort in Switzerland year-round, on either a weekend Sat–Sun or a midweek Wed–Thu schedule, both timed for comfortable Switzerland daytime rather than awkward hours; exact converted timings for each upcoming date appear in the table below. Instruction is in English, the lingua franca of Swiss corporate project work, with an international cohort. Afterwards you get methodical exam application support, mock exams matched to the real standard, and continued trainer access until you pass.

faq.q: How much does certification training cost in Switzerland?
faq.a: The course fee is US$1,295 for PMP and US$695 for CSM, covering the full live online weekend workshop, study materials, mock exams and exam application support. PMI's PMP exam fee (US$425 member / US$675 non-member) is paid separately to PMI; the CSM exam is included in the course fee.

faq.q: Are the classes live or recorded?
faq.a: Every batch is taught live by an instructor over two weekend days — not pre-recorded videos. You can ask questions in real time, work through exercises with the cohort, and reach the trainer afterwards for doubts.

faq.q: Is the certification recognised by employers in Switzerland?
faq.a: Yes. PMP (from PMI) and CSM (from Scrum Alliance) are global credentials recognised identically everywhere, including by employers and recruiters across Switzerland.
